candidate_II.structure: crates/hawking-providers (separate workspace crate depending on hawking-seed-c)

duplications_to_delete: lib.rs `pub use hawking_seed_c as hawking` re-export
lib.rs packs Error enum + Seed(#[from]) bridge + Result alias -> fold into hawking_seed_c::Error
manifest.rs to_seed() projection + mirrored PackManifest core block -> extend PackManifest directly
verify.rs tamper-string m.contains(TAMPERED) coupling -> typed Error::Pack match
adapter.rs dense backbone dup of hawking_seed_c::adapter::build_plan -> generalize seed's builder
rationale: Candidate I is structurally the lower coherent architecture: with no separate crate, NucleusManifest extends PackManifest in place and packs Error folds into seed Error, so a second Pack ABI / second registry is impossible to construct (satisfied structurally, not by discipline). Deletes the entire crate-boundary layer (Provider-trait indirection, Context String plumbing, to_seed projection).
risks: content-addressed pack identity: PackManifest extension MUST be strictly additive (serde(default), excluded from identity block) or every sealed receipt breaks
evidence::KINDS allow-list: new provider kinds (adapter/forge/doctor/metal/speculation/validation/experiment) must be registered or sealed receipts fail
error unification must preserve exact tamper/missing-file semantics (the literal TAMPERED string is the current contract)
adapter multi-arch must not perturb the llama Op order the CPU/Metal bit-identity parity contract depends on; SSM refusal stays fail-closed
reconcile registry.activate vs seed's Machine Admit into ONE activation authority
CLI collapse <=150 LOC must preserve the demo evidence-chain proof as 'hawking debug packs demo'
single_pack_abi_owner: crates/hawking-seed-c/src/pack.rs (PackManifest additively extended with CapabilityKind/Profile/CapabilityDecl/Implementation/Asset/owned_loc; compat stays seed-c-1)
status: DESIGNED - not implemented. Implementation = careful multi-hour Rust refactor deferred (live run active; must not rush an irreversible downstream deletion).
